SPEAKER_00: India postpones test firing of Agni-3 ballistic missile. From Wikinews, the free news source at wikinews.org. The Indian government has once again delayed the testing of the medium-range Agni-3 missile.
SPEAKER_01: The state-run Defense Research and Development Organization had planned to conduct trials later this month. The Agni-3, which has a range of 3,000 kilometers and the ability to carry nuclear warheads, is intended to boost India's nuclear deterrence capabilities to a great extent.
SPEAKER_02: The DRDO, which has often been criticized for its failure to meet deadlines, indicated its disappointment at the government statement. As a public function for the inauguration of the new DRDO headquarters in New Delhi, Chief Controller Essina Rang pointedly said that his team was prepared to launch Agni-3 ballistic missiles. Agni-3 within the week.
SPEAKER_03: Experts have stated that unless India displays its nuclear capabilities more seriously, its nuclear deterrence capabilities will not seem credible. This sound file and all text in the article are released into the public domain.
